title: Cryogenic Equipment Market to Hit $22.96 Billion at 9.3% CAGR through 2030 | MarketsandMarkets™
author: Marketsandmarkets™
description: The global cryogenic equipment market is expected to grow from $14.74 billion in 2025 to $22.96 billion by 2030, at a CAGR of 9.3%. Growth is driven by the transition to clean energy, increased LNG infrastructure investments, and rising demand in Asia Pacific, particularly in China and India. The metallurgy segment is expected to dominate, while key players include Linde, Air Liquide, Air Products, and Chart Industries.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a key market player with strong diversified service portfolio and regional presence, positioned to benefit from growing cryogenic equipment demand driven by clean energy transition and LNG infrastructure expansion.

Key market player with diversified service portfolio, positioned to benefit from growing cryogenic equipment demand in metallurgy, LNG infrastructure, and industrial gas applications across multiple regions.

Major player in cryogenic equipment market with strong regional presence, well-positioned to benefit from rapid market growth driven by LNG investments, hydrogen production, and clean energy initiatives.

Key market player in cryogenic equipment sector, positioned to benefit from growing demand in metallurgy, aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing sectors requiring high-performance cryogenic systems.

Listed as a major player in the cryogenic equipment market with strong global presence, well-positioned to capitalize on the 9.3% CAGR growth and increasing demand across LNG, industrial gases, and healthcare applications.

title: Baker Hughes Successfully Prices $6.5 Billion and €3 Billion Offerings of Senior Notes
author: Na
description: Baker Hughes priced a $6.5 billion U.S. dollar and €3 billion euro debt offering consisting of senior unsecured notes across multiple tranches with maturities ranging from 2029 to 2056. The company intends to use net proceeds to fund a portion of the cash consideration for its proposed acquisition of Chart Industries, Inc. The offerings are expected to close on March 11, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Successfully completed a large debt offering ($6.5B USD + €3B EUR) with favorable pricing across multiple tranches, demonstrating strong market access and investor confidence. The capital raise enables the company to pursue a strategic acquisition, indicating growth ambitions and financial capacity.

Being acquired by Baker Hughes in a cash transaction signals validation of Chart Industries' business value and provides shareholders with liquidity and certainty of value through an established, well-capitalized acquirer.

title: Chart Industries Just Delivered $1 Billion in Adjusted EBITDA, so Why Did One Investor Sell Off $54 Million in Stock?
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: No Street Capital sold 265,000 shares of Chart Industries (worth ~$53.7 million) in Q4 2025, reducing its stake despite the company achieving $1.01 billion in adjusted EBITDA. The sale likely reflects portfolio rebalancing toward higher-growth tech and consumer names, as Chart's 9% annual return underperforms the S&P 500's 17% gain. The pending Baker Hughes acquisition at $210/share also limits upside potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong fundamentals with $1.01B adjusted EBITDA and 21.5% backlog growth, but offset by weak Q4 orders (-23.8% YoY), limited upside due to $210 acquisition price, and underperformance vs. broader market. Insider selling suggests capital rotation rather than fundamental concerns.
Listed as a top holding ($114.39M, 7.7% of AUM) in the portfolio that is rotating away from energy equipment, indicating investor preference for higher-growth consumer/tech names.
Listed as a top holding ($110.74M, 7.5% of AUM) in the portfolio, reflecting investor confidence in this higher-growth financial services company.
Listed as a top holding ($109.91M, 7.4% of AUM) in the portfolio, indicating investor preference for this growth-oriented communications platform company.

title: Chart Industries Exit Comes With a $210 Takeout Clock and $6 Billion Backlog
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Paradice Investment Management fully exited its Chart Industries position, selling 58,813 shares worth $11.77 million in Q4. Chart Industries is being acquired by Baker Hughes at $210 per share, with closing expected mid-2026. Despite strong operational performance—record Q3 orders of $1.68 billion and $6.05 billion backlog—the stock now trades as a merger-arbitrage play with compressed upside and execution risk.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While Chart Industries shows strong operational metrics (record orders, high margins, growing backlog), the pending acquisition at $210/share transforms it into a merger-arbitrage play with limited upside and execution risk through mid-2026. The institutional exit and analyst commentary suggest the risk-reward profile has shifted unfavorably for active investors.

Baker Hughes is acquiring Chart Industries at $210/share, a strategic move in the energy and industrial gas sector. No specific sentiment drivers are provided in the article regarding Baker Hughes' position or performance.

title: Why One Fund Sold $5 Million of Chart Industries Stock
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Connecticut-based Iridian Asset Management sold 23,051 shares of Chart Industries (worth $4.67 million) in Q4, reducing its position from 2.3% to 0.48% of AUM. The sale reflects capital discipline given Chart Industries' pending acquisition by Baker Hughes at $210 per share, which caps the stock's upside potential. Despite strong operational performance with record Q3 orders of $1.68 billion and a $6.05 billion backlog, the fund trimmed the position to reallocate capital to higher-beta holdings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the fund's sale appears negative on the surface, the article frames it as a pragmatic capital allocation decision rather than a conviction shift. Chart Industries shows strong operational fundamentals (record orders, expanding backlog, positive cash flow), but the pending Baker Hughes acquisition at $210/share caps upside potential, making position trimming a disciplined move rather than a bearish signal.

Baker Hughes is acquiring Chart Industries at $210 per share, positioning itself to gain Chart's strong market position in LNG, hydrogen, and carbon capture solutions, which are aligned with energy transition trends.
